name: RxDB
description: RxDB is a local-first, reactive NoSQL database for JavaScript applications. It stores JSON documents locally with schema validation and provides real-time reactive queries via RxJS observables. RxDB runs in browsers, Node.js, Electron, and React Native with pluggable storage backends (Dexie.js, IndexedDB, SQLite, and more). It supports offline-first replication with multiple protocols (HTTP, GraphQL, WebRTC, CouchDB, WebSocket, Firestore, NATS) and includes encryption, multi-tab support, and CRDT-based conflict resolution.
format_on_disk: Pluggable: Dexie.js (default) / IndexedDB / SQLite / LocalStorage / Memory
core_strengths: Reactive queries via RxJS observables — UI automatically updates on data changes
JSON Schema-based validation with migration strategies for schema evolution
Pluggable storage: Dexie.js, IndexedDB, SQLite, LocalStorage, in-memory
Multi-protocol replication: HTTP, GraphQL, WebRTC, CouchDB, WebSocket, NATS
Offline-first with automatic sync, conflict detection, and CRDT-based resolution
Multi-tab support with cross-tab observables via BroadcastChannel
Encryption plugin for field-level document encryption at rest
Runs anywhere JavaScript runs — browsers, Node.js, Electron, React Native
best_use_cases: Offline-first and progressive web apps (PWAs) needing local data with cloud sync
Reactive UIs where data changes must propagate instantly to all components
Multi-device applications with peer-to-peer sync via WebRTC
Electron/React Native apps needing local-first data with optional server sync
Applications needing encrypted client-side storage with schema validation
remarks: RxDB is schemaful by design — all collections require a JSON Schema definition, and the dev-mode plugin enforces validation. Storage is pluggable: Dexie.js (default, works everywhere), IndexedDB, SQLite (premium), LocalStorage, in-memory, and FoundationDB among others. The storage layer is abstracted behind the RxStorage interface so you can swap backends without changing application code. Replication plugins (HTTP, GraphQL, CouchDB, WebRTC, etc.) are opt-in and must be imported separately. Some premium features (SQLite storage, Query Builder plugin, Vector Search) require a paid license.
